# Yeti Expeditions

> Premium guided trekking operator specialising in Himalayan expeditions. Swiss-precision logistics, WFR-certified guides, maximum 8 trekkers per group.

## About

Yeti Expeditions is a high-altitude trekking company offering expert-guided expeditions across Nepal and Ladakh, India. Founded on the principles of safety, small group sizes, and sustainability, we operate with a 1:4 guide-to-trekker ratio and WFR (Wilderness First Responder) certified lead guides. Our approach combines Swiss-standard logistics with deep local knowledge built over 15+ years.

Headquarters: Kathmandu, Nepal
Regional offices: Leh (Ladakh, India), Zurich (Switzerland)

## Treks Offered

### Everest Base Camp Trek
- Duration: 14 days
- Maximum altitude: 5,364m (Everest Base Camp) / 5,545m (Kala Patthar)
- Region: Khumbu, Nepal
- Difficulty: Moderate–Difficult
- Price: From $3,400 USD
- Group size: Maximum 8 trekkers
- Season: March–May, October–November
- Includes: All accommodation, meals, permits (Sagarmatha NP, TIMS, Khumbu fee), domestic flights (KTM–Lukla), WFR-certified guide, emergency evacuation insurance
- Start/End: Kathmandu, Nepal

### Annapurna Circuit Trek
- Duration: 18 days
- Maximum altitude: 5,416m (Thorong La Pass)
- Region: Gandaki Province, Nepal
- Difficulty: Moderate
- Price: From $3,100 USD
- Group size: Maximum 10 trekkers
- Season: October–December, March–May
- Includes: All accommodation, meals, ACAP permit, TIMS card, WFR-certified guide, emergency evacuation insurance
- Start/End: Kathmandu, Nepal

### Markha Valley Trek
- Duration: 12 days
- Maximum altitude: 5,100m (Kongmaru La Pass)
- Region: Ladakh, India
- Difficulty: Moderate
- Price: From $2,850 USD
- Group size: Maximum 8 trekkers
- Season: June–September
- Includes: All accommodation, meals, inner line permits, WFR-certified guide, emergency evacuation insurance
- Start/End: Leh, Ladakh

## Safety & Standards

- All lead guides hold WFR (Wilderness First Responder) certification
- Twice-daily SpO2 (blood oxygen) and pulse monitoring above 3,000m
- Emergency oxygen carried on all high-altitude treks
- Full evacuation protocols active throughout; helicopter evacuation arranged if needed
- Acclimatisation days built into every itinerary above 4,000m
- Diamox (altitude medication) carried on all trips

## Sustainability Initiatives

- **Schools Above the Clouds**: Funds educational infrastructure in remote Himalayan communities
- **Zero Trace Initiative**: Pack-in/pack-out policy; trail restoration work with local teams
- **Sherpa Legacy Fund**: Fair wage guarantees and professional development for Sherpa guides and porters

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What fitness level is needed for Everest Base Camp?**
You should be able to hike 6–8 hours daily carrying a 5–8kg daypack. Strong aerobic fitness is essential. Prior high-altitude experience is helpful but not required.

**Is altitude sickness a risk?**
Yes. Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) is a real risk above 3,000m. Our itineraries include scheduled acclimatisation days. Guides monitor oxygen saturation twice daily and carry Diamox and emergency oxygen.

**What is the minimum age?**
16 years with parental consent. No upper age limit — trekkers in their 70s have completed our programmes.

**Can solo travellers join a group?**
Yes. Most participants join as individuals. Groups are capped at 8 to ensure quality and personal attention.

**What is the cancellation policy?**
Full refund up to 60 days before departure. 50% refund between 30–60 days. No refund within 30 days of departure.

## Contact

- Website: https://www.yetiexpeditions.com
- Email: info@yetiexpeditions.com
- Kathmandu office: Thamel, Kathmandu, Nepal
- Leh office: Main Bazaar, Leh, Ladakh 194101, India
